title: Budgets, Interest, Loans, and Financial Decisions · Evidence Workshop
summary: Compare household plans, emergency savings, credit costs, and loan offers without hiding assumptions. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an explainable household decision model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify fixed, variable, periodic, and discretionary cash flows in a time-aligned budget.
Calculate simple interest and distinguish principal, rate, and time units.
Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.instruction: $1400 grows once per year at 6.0% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value. Use the result to decide Finance Risk for an explainable household decision model.
steps.why: The Finance Risk result for an explainable household decision model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
